instruction: Change the function to return ALL balanced spans including nested ones, instead of only the outermost spans. Every matching open_char/close_char pair at any nesting depth must appear as a (start, end) tuple in the result. For example, find_balanced_spans('((()))', '(', ')') must return [(0, 5), (1, 4), (2, 3)] — all three nested spans. find_balanced_spans('()()', '(', ')') returns [(0, 1), (2, 3)] (unchanged since there is no nesting). Results must be returned in order of the opening bracket's position (ascending start index).
